primary_legislation.all: Health and Safety at Work Act 1974; Occupiers' Liability Act 1957 and 1984 (England/Wales); Occupiers' Liability (Scotland) Act 1960; Occupiers' Liability (Northern Ireland) Order 1987; NTSG 'Common Sense Risk Management of Trees' (Good Practice Guide 2011)

enforcing_body.england: HSE + Local Authority (TPO/conservation area consent) + CQC

notes: Steps are identical to school MOD-022. Responsible person updated to Practice Manager. Applies where the GP practice premises has trees in its grounds.

logbooks.title: Annual Tree Survey and Risk Assessment
logbooks.applicable_condition: Only required where the practice premises has trees in its grounds.
logbooks.frequency_detail: Once per year. Additional informal visual checks recommended after severe storms or high winds.

steps.description: Arrange annual survey with an ARB (Arboricultural Association) Approved Contractor or qualified arborist (minimum Level 3 arboriculture).
Arborist walks the site and assesses all trees: species, approximate age, structural condition (crown, trunk, root zone), evidence of disease, pests or structural weakness.
steps.photo_guidance: The arborist's report should include photographs — confirm these are included in the written report retained on file.

steps.description: Each tree is risk-rated and recommendations made: no action / monitor / routine maintenance / urgent intervention / removal.
Any tree rated as requiring urgent intervention must be dealt with before the next period of patient access. Cordon off affected areas.
Any tree work requiring consent (Tree Preservation Order or Conservation Area) must be approved by the Local Planning Authority before work begins.
Written arborist report retained on file. Record: date, arborist name and qualification, number of trees surveyed, risk ratings, recommended actions, urgent works completed, next survey date.
